question.text: For a trust, which of the following must be identified as beneficial owners under the IFSCA Guidelines?

answer.text: The author of the trust, the trustee, beneficiaries with 10% or more interest, and any other natural person exercising ultimate effective control
Only the trustee and the named beneficiaries
Only the author (settlor) of the trust
Only beneficiaries with more than 50% interest in the trust

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For trusts, beneficial owner identification includes: (i) the author of the trust; (ii) the trustee; (iii) beneficiaries with 10% or more interest in the trust; and (iv) any other natural person exercising ultimate effective control through a chain of control or ownership.
**The Trap:** The threshold for trust beneficiaries is 10% or more (not 15% as mentioned for initial identification elsewhere — note the Guidelines have specific provisions). ALL four categories must be identified, not just one or two.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Trust beneficial ownership identification is critical in IFSCA-01 because many international structures use trusts. Series XXIV covers trusts in domestic context but IFSCA-01 deals with offshore trusts common in IFSC.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For trusts, remember four categories: Author (settlor), Trustee, Beneficiaries (10%+), and Controllers. This is frequently tested because trusts are commonly used in international finance.
